inequality: H_fixed + Veff * H_per_req + H_control < B_fixed + V * B_per_req + B_control

special_points.Veff_equals_0: no accepted fast-path credit; reopen requires H_fixed + H_control < B_fixed + V * B_per_req + B_control without per-request hybrid amortization
special_points.V_equals_0: zero request volume leaves no workload amortization; fixed/control terms dominate under current architecture boundary
special_points.H_per_req_ge_B_per_req: if hybrid per-request cost is no better than baseline, increasing accepted fast-path volume cannot create a per-request advantage
special_points.H_fixed_gt_0: positive fixed substrate cost requires nonzero useful volume or lower non-volume terms before a finite threshold can exist
special_points.fallback_frequency_equals_1: all fallback implies Veff = 0, so accepted fast-path volume cannot reopen the claim

solved_condition: V >= 0 && HperReq < 0 && Hcontrol + Hfixed + HperReq*Veff < Bcontrol + Bfixed + BperReq*V
